screens.name: DriverDashboardPage
screens.role: delivery_driver (guarded by RoleGuard allow=["delivery_driver"], wrapped in DriverLayout)
screens.purpose: Driver home screen showing the day's KPIs and an earnings summary, plus quick-jump shortcuts to available orders and assigned orders.
features: Read-only KPI dashboard (no order actions here)

Four StatCards: Ganancias hoy (earnings.today fallback stats.earnings_today), Entregas hoy (earnings.deliveries_today fallback stats.deliveries_completed), Rating (stats.average_rating toFixed(1), shows '—' if null), Eficiencia (stats.efficiency%)
Earnings summary card (rendered only when earnings loaded): Hoy / Esta semana / Este mes / Total via formatCurrency
Two shortcut cards linking to /delivery/available and /delivery/my-orders
No accept/reject, no status updates, no availability toggle, no GPS reporting originated on this page
api_calls: GET /dashboard/driver (getDriverDashboard) -> DriverDashboard {deliveries_today, deliveries_completed, earnings_today, average_rating, punctuality, satisfaction, efficiency}
GET /delivery/earnings (getEarnings) -> EarningsSummary {today, this_week, this_month, total, deliveries_today, deliveries_total}
Both fired together via Promise.all on mount (useEffect [])
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Heading 'Bienvenido 🍗' / subtitle 'Tu día en Chikenhot'. 2-col grid of StatCards. Centered Loader2 spinner while loading; red error banner on failure. StatCard tones: success (green) for earnings, warning (yellow) for rating.

screens.name: DriverAvailablePage
screens.purpose: List of unassigned nearby orders the driver can claim; primary place to ACCEPT an order.
features: Accept order: handleAccept(id) calls acceptOrder then navigates to /delivery/my-orders/:id; per-row accepting spinner state; on error shows message and reloads list (no explicit reject action — drivers simply do not accept)
Manual refresh button (RefreshCw) re-runs load()
Each order card shows: order_number, created_at (formatDate), delivery_fee labelled 'Ganancia', delivery_address (MapPin), payment_method (capitalized, CreditCard) and total
Count header: '{orders.length} esperando un repartidor'
Empty state '🕑 No hay pedidos disponibles ahora mismo'; no status updates / availability toggle / GPS here
api_calls: GET /delivery/nearby-orders (getNearbyOrders) -> NearbyOrder[] {id, order_number, status, delivery_address, total, delivery_fee, payment_method, created_at}
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Loader2 spinner (h-40) while loading. Red error banner above list. Cards use .card class; full-width btn-primary 'Aceptar pedido' disabled while that row is accepting.

screens.name: DriverMyOrdersPage
screens.purpose: Lists the driver's own orders, split into Active and History sections, each row linking to the order detail.
features: Client-side split: active = status not in [delivered, canceled]; past = status in [delivered, canceled]
Active section header with count; empty state with Package icon + link to /delivery/available
History section (only if past.length>0) shows up to 20 past orders (past.slice(0,20))
OrderRow shows order_number, OrderStatusBadge, created_at, delivery_fee labelled 'delivery', ChevronRight; links to /delivery/my-orders/:id
No accept/reject, status update, availability toggle, or GPS on this page
api_calls: GET /orders (listMyOrders) -> {orders: Order[]} (backend scopes to the authenticated driver's orders)
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Heading 'Mis pedidos'. Loader2 spinner (h-64) while loading; red error banner on failure. Rows are .card links with hover shadow.

screens.name: DriverOrderDetailPage
screens.purpose: Full detail + action screen for a single assigned order: navigation map, customer/contact info, items, status timeline, and the status-progression action buttons.
features: Status updates (driver workflow): pickUp() when status==='ready' -> PATCH /orders/:id/status with status 'on_the_way' (button 'Recoger y salir en ruta'); complete() when status==='on_the_way' -> PATCH /delivery/complete/:id (button 'Marcar como entregado'). Buttons render conditionally on canPickUp/canDeliver

Auto-refresh polling: window.setInterval every 10000ms (10s) re-fetches getOrder while order is active; stops when status in [delivered, canceled] (so driver sees admin marking order 'ready' without manual refresh). aliveRef guards against setState after unmount
Embedded LiveOrderMap (refreshSeconds=10, height 260) wrapped in ErrorBoundary, only when delivery_address contains embedded (lat,lon) matching COORDS_RE /\((-?\d+\.\d+),\s*(-?\d+\.\d+)\)/
Deep links 'Abrir en Google Maps' (maps/dir destination=lat,lon travelmode=driving) and 'Abrir en Waze' (waze.com/ul?ll=lat,lon&navigate=yes)
Customer card: name, tap-to-call tel: link for customer_phone, delivery_address, payment_method (special orange 'Cobrar {total}' hint when payment_method==='efectivo'), notes banner
Products list (quantity × product_name, subtotal) and Total a cobrar
Timeline (order.timeline) rendered as vertical checklist with formatted timestamps
No accept/reject here (accept happens on available/map pages); no availability toggle; no driver-GPS reporting originates here (location reporting lives in DriverLayout)
api_calls: GET /orders/{id} (getOrder) -> Order — on mount and on each 10s poll
PATCH /delivery/complete/{id} (completeDelivery) for delivery completion; followed by a fresh GET /orders/{id} to refresh timeline
Indirectly via LiveOrderMap: GET /orders/{id}/tracking (getOrderTracking) to render driver position/route
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Back link to 'Mis pedidos'. Header with order_number + OrderStatusBadge. Action buttons are full-width btn-primary with Truck/CheckCircle2 icons and a Loader2 while updating. Loader2 spinner (h-64) while initial load; error/not-found state offers a 'Volver' link.

screens.name: DriverMapPage
screens.purpose: Leaflet map of nearby unassigned orders with proximity filtering and quick accept; an alternative spatial view of the available list.
features: Accept order from map popup or from the bottom shortlist (top 5): handleAccept -> acceptOrder then navigate to /delivery/my-orders/:id; per-order accepting state

'Mi ubicación' (Crosshair) uses browser navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition({enableHighAccuracy:true, timeout:10000}) one-shot to set myPos (this is map-centering only, NOT the background GPS reporting)
Distance annotation via Haversine from myPos; radius filter slider 1–20 km (default 10) filters and sorts visible orders by distance
Marker pin color by urgency: <2km green, <5km orange, else red; driver shown with blue 'me' marker; FlyToOnce only re-centers when position moves >~50m to avoid jumpiness
Order parsing: only orders whose delivery_address matches COORDS_RE are placed on the map
Manual refresh (RefreshCw) re-loads nearby orders; popup and list show order_number, address (first segment split on ' — '), distance, delivery_fee
No status updates, no availability toggle, no driver-GPS reporting (one-shot locate only)
api_calls: Browser Geolocation API getCurrentPosition (client-side only; does NOT hit backend)
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Heading '🗺️ Pedidos cerca'. 420px map with BaseTileLayer + TileStyleSwitcher. DEFAULT_CENTER Lima [-12.0464,-77.0428] when no position. Loading overlay spinner over map; red error banner. Hint to enable location when myPos null. Bottom list capped at 5 cards.

screens.name: DriverEarningsPage
screens.purpose: Detailed earnings breakdown for the driver across time windows plus delivery totals.
features: Earnings breakdown (read-only): hero card 'Total acumulado' (data.total) with '{deliveries_total} entregas completadas'
StatCards: Hoy (data.today, hint '{deliveries_today} entregas'), Esta semana (this_week), Este mes (this_month), Total entregas (deliveries_total)
Footnote: earnings are computed from delivered orders
No accept/reject, status updates, availability toggle, or GPS
api_calls: GET /delivery/earnings (getEarnings) -> EarningsSummary {today, this_week, this_month, total, deliveries_today, deliveries_total}
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Heading '💰 Ganancias'. Gradient brand hero card (from-brand-500 to-brand-700) with large total. 2-col StatCard grid. Loader2 spinner (h-64); red error banner on failure.

screens.name: DriverRatingsPage
screens.purpose: Shows the driver's received customer ratings: average, star distribution, and recent reviews.
features: Ratings view (read-only): computes avg, total, and 5→1 star distribution (count + pct) client-side from reviews via useMemo
Summary card: big average (toFixed(1)), StarRating(readonly) of rounded average, review count, and per-star distribution bars
Recent reviews list sorted by created_at desc: StarRating, formatted date, optional comment, 'Pedido #{order_id}'
Empty state 'Aún no has recibido calificaciones' when no reviews
Depends on userId from useAuthStore; effect skips if userId missing
No accept/reject, status updates, availability toggle, or GPS
api_calls: GET /reviews/driver/{driverId} (listDriverReviews, driverId = auth store userId) -> Review[] {id, order_id, customer_id, driver_id, rating, comment, created_at, customer_name}
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Heading '⭐ Mis calificaciones'. Yellow star distribution bars. Loader2 spinner (h-64); red error banner. Uses StarRating component (readonly).

screens.name: DriverRegisterPage
screens.route: /register-driver (also linked as /register-driver; standalone, NOT inside DriverLayout / no RoleGuard)
screens.role: public / unauthenticated (driver self-signup; creates a delivery_driver account)
screens.purpose: 4-step wizard to onboard a new delivery driver, collecting account, personal/emergency/address, vehicle/documents, and bank details.
features: Multi-step stepper (4 steps: Cuenta, Personal, Vehículo, Banco) with progress indicator and per-step client-side validation
Step1 validation: full_name >=3 chars, valid email regex, password >=6, password === confirm_password, phone >=6
Step2 validation: document_id >=6, birth_date required; gender radios; home address/district; emergency contact
Step3 validation: vehicle_type required; vehicle_plate required (>=3) unless vehicle_type==='bicicleta'; vehicle brand/model/year/color; optional license + insurance (number/expiry)
Step4: bank name, account type (ahorros/corriente), account number, CCI, account holder
Submit builds DriverRegisterPayload (omitting empty optionals), registers, stores session via useAuthStore.setSession, and hard-redirects to defaultHomeForRole(role) (i.e. /delivery)
Back/Continue navigation; final submit button shows Loader2 + 'Creando cuenta...'; links to /login and customer /register
No order actions / GPS / earnings — registration only
screens.ui_notes: Spanish UI. Centered card (max-w-3xl). 🛵 hero, 'Únete como repartidor'. Vehicle/gender/bank-type rendered as radio 'chip' selectors. Blue reassurance note about bank data on step 4. Red error banner per step.

routing_notes: All driver app screens are nested under Route path=/delivery guarded by RoleGuard allow=["delivery_driver"], with DriverLayout as the shared shell (App.tsx lines 75-85). Children: index=DriverDashboardPage, available, map, my-orders, my-orders/:id, earnings, ratings.

DriverRegisterPage is a separate top-level route /register-driver (App.tsx line 90) with NO RoleGuard and NO DriverLayout — it is the public self-signup. defaultHomeForRole('delivery_driver') returns '/delivery' (RoleGuard.tsx).

DriverLayout provides the sticky header (brand, availability toggle, logout), the 'Hola, {fullName}' line, and a fixed bottom nav: Inicio (/delivery end), Mapa (/delivery/map), Pedidos (/delivery/my-orders), Ganancias (/delivery/earnings), Rating (/delivery/ratings). Note: /delivery/available is reachable only via dashboard/my-orders links, not the bottom nav.

Availability toggle lives ONLY in DriverLayout (header Power button): POST /delivery/toggle-availability (toggleAvailability) -> {is_available, message}. Initial 'available' state is optimistically set to true on mount (not fetched from backend). Label flips between 'Disponible' (green) and 'Offline' (gray).

BACKGROUND GPS REPORTING (the key mechanism, lives in DriverLayout via useDriverLocationReporter, NOT in any individual page): DriverLayout calls useDriverLocationReporter(available === true, 15). WHEN ACTIVE: only while enabled === true, i.e. while the driver's availability is 'Disponible'; toggling Offline disables it (effect cleanup cancels the loop). INTERVAL: 15 seconds, scheduled with a RECURSIVE setTimeout (not setInterval) so the next tick is scheduled only after the previous report completes — this prevents overlapping getCurrentPosition calls when GPS is slow. CADENCE PER TICK: tick() => reportReal(); if reportReal returns false, reportFallback(); then schedule next tick in intervalSeconds*1000 ms.

GPS PAYLOAD & ENDPOINT: both real and fallback reporting call updateDriverLocation (services/tracking.ts) => PATCH /delivery/location with body {latitude, longitude, zone?}. Real path: navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition with options {enableHighAccuracy:true, timeout:8000, maximumAge:30000}; payload = {latitude: pos.coords.latitude, longitude: pos.coords.longitude} (no zone). FALLBACK (when navigator.geolocation absent or getCurrentPosition errors/returns false): seeds a random point near Lima base [-12.0464,-77.0428] (±0.01), then jitters by ±0.0004 each tick and posts {latitude, longitude, zone:'Lima Centro (demo)'} so the demo keeps producing movement.

Distinct from background reporting: DriverMapPage's 'Mi ubicación' and DriverAvailablePage have NO background reporting; the map's getCurrentPosition (timeout 10000) is a one-shot used only for centering/distance filtering and does NOT PATCH /delivery/location.

Driver order status workflow (DriverOrderDetailPage): accept happens earlier (POST /delivery/accept/:id on available/map pages) -> when admin sets order to 'ready', driver presses 'Recoger y salir en ruta' = PATCH /orders/:id/status {status:'on_the_way'} -> then 'Marcar como entregado' = PATCH /delivery/complete/:id. The detail page polls GET /orders/:id every 10s while active so the driver sees the admin's 'ready' transition without manual refresh.

LiveOrderMap (embedded in DriverOrderDetailPage with refreshSeconds=10) consumes GET /orders/:id/tracking which returns driver_latitude/longitude/zone/updated_at — i.e. it surfaces exactly the position data pushed by useDriverLocationReporter, closing the loop between the driver's PATCH /delivery/location reports and the customer/driver-facing live map.
